title: Bee Removal in Kips Bay, Manhattan | $350-$550 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in Kips Bay, Manhattan. Safe, humane extraction $350-$550. Serving residential & commercial properties. Call Best Pest Control NYC today!
intro: Best Pest Control NYC provides expert bee removal services throughout Kips Bay, Manhattan, protecting homes and businesses from unwanted bee colonies. Our licensed technicians safely remove bees from residential buildings, co-ops, and commercial properties between East 23rd and East 34th Streets. With our humane extraction methods and competitive pricing of $350-$550, we ensure your Kips Bay property remains bee-free.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Kips Bay residents and businesses for years with fast response times, humane bee relocation practices, and thorough follow-up inspections. Our technicians understand the unique challenges of Manhattan properties, from pre-war walk-ups to modern high-rises, and deliver effective solutions tailored to your building's specific needs.
localContext: Kips Bay's mix of historic brownstones, post-war apartment complexes, and proximity to NYU Langone Medical Center creates diverse environments where bees may establish hives in facades, rooftops, and courtyard gardens. The tree-lined residential streets and community gardens near the East River make this neighborhood particularly attractive to foraging bees seeking nesting sites.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to a bee problem in Kips Bay?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service for bee removal in Kips Bay, Manhattan. Our team understands the urgency of bee infestations, especially in densely populated residential areas, and we prioritize rapid response to protect residents and pets from potential stings.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 bee removal service include?
faqs.answer: Our bee removal service includes a thorough inspection to locate the hive, safe extraction of the colony, removal of honeycomb and debris, treatment of the affected area to prevent re-infestation, and sealing of entry points. The final cost depends on hive size, location accessibility, and the complexity of removal.

faqs.question: Do you kill the bees or relocate them?
faqs.answer: Whenever possible, we use humane relocation methods to preserve bee populations, which are vital pollinators. Our technicians work with local beekeepers to safely transfer colonies to appropriate apiaries. In cases where relocation isn't feasible due to safety concerns or aggressive species, we use targeted removal methods as a last resort.

faqs.question: Can bees return after removal in Kips Bay buildings?
faqs.answer: Bees are unlikely to return once we've completely removed the hive and sealed entry points. Our service includes thorough cleanup of pheromone traces that might attract new swarms, and we seal cracks, gaps, and openings in your building's exterior to prevent future bee colonies from establishing themselves in the same location.
